articleHeadline: Redefining How Small Businesses Build, Grow, and Get Seen

st2LeftSections.sub: What the Industry Needs to Rethink
paras:
Danielle believes the design and digital industries need to stop treating small business clients as if they are less sophisticated simply because they have smaller budgets.
Many entrepreneurs do not need bigger campaigns. They need better foundations.
They need messaging that makes sense. Websites that are easy to navigate. Offers that are clearly explained. 
 Trust signals that reassure cautious buyers. SEO basics that are set up properly. Systems that reduce admin instead of adding to it.
Visual identities that feel aligned with the quality of the work being delivered.
They also need agencies and designers who are willing to educate, not just execute.
This is especially important as more founders turn to DIY tools and AI platforms. 
The industry has an opportunity to move from being a gatekeeper to being a guide.
For Danielle, the question is not “Will AI replace designers?”
The better question is: “Will designers become more valuable because business owners now need help making smarter decisions with more tools than ever before?”
Flow Cre8tive’s answer is yes.
The future belongs to agencies that can combine creativity with commercial sense, technology with empathy, and execution with education.

st2RightSections.sub: A Future Built Around Access
paras:
Looking ahead, Danielle sees Flow Cre8tive expanding its role as both a service provider and an educational platform. The vision is to continue building a place where business owners can access the tools, resources and support they need at different stages of growth. Some will want a full custom website and branding project. Others will need a VIP design day, a mini makeover, a website audit, templates, launch documents or practical guidance they can implement themselves.

This layered approach reflects the reality of small business. Not everyone needs the same level of support at the same time.But everyone deserves access to quality thinking. Flow Cre8tive is also exploring how AI education and consulting can help clients work smarter, streamline operations and make better use of emerging tools without losing their voice, values or customer connection.

The studio’s future is not about becoming bigger for the sake of scale.It is about becoming more useful. More resources. More clarity. More pathways. More ways to help founders stop spinning in circles and start building with confidence.

st2RightSections.sub: The Real Work of Design
paras:
At its best, design is not surface-level. It is a bridge. A bridge between confusion and clarity. Between idea and income. Between a founder’s private ambition and the public presence that allows others to believe in it too.
That is the work Flow Cre8tive has been doing since its first crisis-born project in 2020. The studio has helped hundreds of people launch, refine and grow businesses that may otherwise have stayed stuck in notebooks, voice memos, half-built websites or “one day” conversations.

Danielle Mountford’s journey is a reminder that creativity becomes powerful when it is placed in service of people. Not just brands. People. People who need a way forward. People who are brave enough to start. People who deserve to be seen clearly, taken seriously and supported properly. Flow Cre8tive’s work proves that small business design does not have to be small in ambition. It can be strategic, thoughtful, beautifully executed and deeply human and in a noisy digital world, that might be the most valuable kind of design there is.

articleHeadline: Inside Podium Creative: The Women Driving Its Next Chapter

st2LeftSections.sub: A Leadership Transition That Defines the Next Chapter
paras: Every agency has defining moments, and for Podium Creative, one of the most significant is its leadership transition.

Founder and Managing Director Andy Thevarokiam has stepped back from day-to-day leadership after building the SEO, PPC, and social media management firm over many years. This shift marks a natural evolution in Podium’s journey.
The challenge was not change itself, but continuity—ensuring that the agency’s values, performance standards, and client relationships remained stable through the transition.
The solution came from within the organisation.

Podium is now led by three co-owners: Sarah McKevitt, Lia Gordon, and Katie Toman. Each has been deeply involved in the agency’s growth and operations, making this transition one of continuity rather than disruption.

Their leadership ensures that Podium moves forward with consistency, supported by people who already understand the business inside out. The outcome is stability with momentum, three leaders stepping into ownership with clarity, alignment, and readiness.
One key lesson from this journey is that the strongest transitions are built on trust developed over time, where leadership is not introduced but grown.

st2RightSections.sub: Small Team. Big Impact. Real Relationships.
paras: At Podium, being a small team is a deliberate strength. It allows for closer collaboration, faster decision-making, and deeper client relationships built on trust and consistency.

Rather than functioning as a distant agency, Podium operates as an extension of each client’s business. Every project is handled with ownership, ensuring that outcomes are shared and accountability is clear.

The team itself is a mix of creative thinkers and analytical strategists. Each member brings a different perspective, but all are aligned on one principle—marketing should be transparent, measurable, and effective.
Every decision is guided by a simple question: does this drive real business growth?

st2RightSections.sub: Behind the Scenes: Culture, People, and How Podium Works
paras: Behind Podium’s work is a culture built on trust, clarity, and collaboration.

The agency describes itself as trustworthy and female-led, with leadership actively involved in both strategic direction and day-to-day execution. This creates a close-knit environment where decisions are shared and accountability is collective.
The team operates as a unified group rather than separate functions. Strategy, creative, and performance teams work together continuously, ensuring campaigns are shaped by multiple perspectives.
This integration allows Podium to balance creativity with structure—ensuring ideas are not only innovative but also measurable and effective.
Even as leadership evolves, the culture remains consistent—steady, collaborative, and performance-driven.

articleHeadline: BIOGENA's SEO GROWTH JOURNEY WITH GET ON TOP
visionParas: Sharing one of team get on top gmbh’s project journeys and wins their collaboration with BIOGENA, an international company based in Austria, has combined premium micronutrients with a holistic understanding of health for over 20 years. With evidence-based solutions, innovative health concepts and the ambition to make wellbeing tangible, BIOGENA supports people on their journey to a better quality of life.
What makes BIOGENA’s approach distinctive is its strong focus on its own online shop, allowing the brand to maintain complete control over its customer experience, communication, and brand presence.

In an industry filled with strong competition across both transactional and informational spaces, BIOGENA was competing alongside large online pharmacies, supplement brands, health portals and specialized blogs.

While the brand had already established itself as a quality leader, the opportunity lay in strengthening its digital visibility and making its expertise more discoverable online. Following a comprehensive SEO audit, the get on top team identified that the strongest potential was within the content itself. The existing content already carried depth and quality, but there was room to position it more strategically where it could create the greatest impact.
Together, the teams developed a carefully structured content strategy focused on aligning expertise with search intent while bringing greater clarity between shop-focused and editorial content.

Since then, BIOGENA's in-house SEO team and science experts have worked closely with get on top's SEO specialists to create content that is both scientifically grounded and strategically optimized for search visibility.

A major milestone in the journey came with the launch of BIOGENA’s new online shop in February 2024. From the very beginning of the relaunch process, get on top supported the project with strategic SEO input, helping create a smooth transition, strong rankings from day one, and impressive momentum.

The collaboration led to a 190% increase in organic revenue, a 350% rise in clicks, and a 400% growth in visibility. Alongside this growth, BIOGENA continues to strengthen its presence and an expanding product range, all driven by the shared vision of helping more people live a fantastic life.
